facts_disclosed: Switched to Toyota Aqua hybrid, petrol savings $280->$110/month

turns.content: i made a big purchase this week and i'm having that thing where you spend a lot of money and then immediately start second-guessing yourself even though you know it was the right call. can you talk me off the ledge eh

Post-purchase anxiety is incredibly common, especially with big purchases. The doubt doesn't necessarily mean it was the wrong decision — it usually just means it was a significant one. What did you buy?

a car. well not new new, second hand. i finally let go of my Mazda and got a Toyota Aqua hybrid. the Mazda had been good to me but the petrol costs were just not sustainable anymore, not with how much driving i do for work across canterbury

A Toyota Aqua hybrid is a really practical choice, especially if you're doing a lot of driving. The fuel savings on a hybrid compared to a petrol Mazda can be dramatic. How much were you spending on petrol before, and what are you expecting with the Aqua?

so with the Mazda i was spending around two hundred and eighty dollars a month on petrol. i'm a midwife and i'm driving out to akaroa, rangiora, kaiapoi, sometimes further, all hours of the day and night for births. the fuel just ate into everything. i've only had the Aqua for about a week but based on what i've been using so far i reckon my monthly petrol will drop to about a hundred and ten dollars. that's a massive difference

That's a saving of roughly $170 a month — over $2,000 a year. That's significant money, especially when you're self-employed and every cost comes out of your own pocket. Over even three or four years of owning the car, that fuel saving alone could add up to $6,000-8,000.

So what's driving the second-guessing? Is it the upfront cost of the car, or something else?

partly the upfront cost, it was more than i wanted to spend. and partly just sentimental rubbish about the Mazda. that car saw me through my divorce, through maia growing up, through a thousand middle-of-the-night drives to births. my birth kit lived in the boot permanently. it felt like giving away a piece of my life even though rationally i know it's just a car eh

That's not rubbish at all — cars absorb a lot of life. The Mazda wasn't just transport, it was your mobile office, your constant companion through some significant years. It makes complete sense to feel that loss even when the upgrade is objectively the right move.

But here's the thing: the Aqua is going to build its own story. The first birth you drive to in it, the first time you load your kit into the back, the first 3am callout in the rain — it'll start becoming yours. And every time you fill up and it costs half what it used to, you'll be reminded that this was the smart call.

Have you set up the boot space for your birth kit already?

yeah first thing i did. the Aqua is smaller than the Mazda which i was worried about but actually the boot is well designed and the kit fits better because i had to rethink the layout. and honestly it handles the hills better than i expected too. the drive over to akaroa has that big hill section and the Mazda used to drink petrol going up there. the Aqua just switches to electric on the downhill and regenerates the battery so by the time you're in akaroa the fuel gauge has barely moved. and it's so quiet which is nice when i'm driving home exhausted at five in the morning after a birth

The regenerative braking on those hills is exactly where a hybrid shines — a route like the Akaroa road with all those climbs and descents is basically the ideal use case. And the quiet cabin on those tired drives home is an underrated benefit. Less noise fatigue means you arrive home in better shape.

So let me reflect this back to you: you've got a car that fits your birth kit, handles your rural routes comfortably, saves you $170 a month in petrol, drives quietly for those post-birth journeys, and handles the Canterbury hills well. What exactly is there to second-guess?

ha okay when you put it all together like that the answer is nothing. i think i just needed to say it all out loud and hear that it makes sense. the Mazda served its purpose and now the Aqua is the right car for the practice i'm running now. i'm going to stop feeling guilty about it and just enjoy the savings eh

Exactly. The guilt is just your brain catching up with a decision your budget already made for you. Put that $170 a month somewhere you can see it accumulating — even just a separate savings account — and in a year when you look at the balance, you'll never question it again.

The Mazda did its job through a hard chapter. The Aqua starts the next one. Take it on a night callout and by the time you get home in the quiet at dawn, it'll feel like yours.

i love that, starting the next chapter. and the savings account idea is smart, i might actually do that. put it toward something nice for me and maia at the end of the year. thanks for this, you've properly settled my brain. kia kaha

Kia kaha. The brain settles when the numbers make sense and the heart catches up. Give it a month of those quiet dawn drives and cheap fill-ups, and the Aqua will feel like it was always yours. Enjoy the road.
